claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have children. I'm inquiring about my two kids - Alex, who's 12, and Blake, who's 8. They both live with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know about Alex (12) and Blake (8), and that they both live with you at home.

To continue assessing your eligibility, can you confirm that you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Now I need to ask: are you the person primarily responsible for Alex and Blake? In other words, do you provide for their day-to-day needs such as food, clothes, and accommodation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. I look after them day-to-day - provide their meals, clothes, and everything they need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Are either Alex or Blake currently in hospital, care, or any residential accommodation for an extended period?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are. They're both at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you. One more question: is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, no one else is claiming for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Here's the summary:

**For Alex (age 12):**
✓ Eligible - Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for their day-to-day care, they're not in hospital or care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

**For Blake (age 8):**
✓ Eligible - Blake is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for their day-to-day care, they're not in hospital or care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

You meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. If you haven't already claimed, you can proceed with making a claim for both children.

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You can use the Child Benefit tax calculator to check if this applies to you.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 12 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for Alex's day-to-day care including food, clothes, and accommodation. Alex is not in hospital or care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

Blake is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for Blake's day-to-day care including food, clothes, and accommodation. Blake is not in hospital or care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. All eligibility criteria are met.

Both children are under 16 years old, live with the claimant, are not in hospital or care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them. The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both children.
